component_identification.content: Base components: 1 game board, 121 Intrigue cards, 100+ Quest cards (starter/plot/mandatory), 11 building tiles, 5 player mats, 30 agents (6 per player color), adventurer cubes in 4 classes (fighter/rogue/cleric/wizard), coin tokens, victory point markers, first-player marker, and rulebook.

core_game_loop.content: Game lasts 8 rounds. In player order, place one agent on an open building space to gain its reward or action. When all agents are placed, play ends round with reassignment: retrieve agents, reveal next round, and resolve upkeep. Complete quests by spending required adventurers/resources for immediate rewards and VP.

detailed_rules.content: At setup each player receives a hidden Lord card that awards endgame bonuses for specific quest categories or buildings. Spaces are either basic board buildings or purchased buildings added during play; owner gains bonus when others use owned buildings. Intrigue cards are played when visiting Waterdeep Harbor or when cards specify timing; Harbor agents stay until round end then get reassigned in order. Mandatory Quests must be completed before any other quest by affected player. Plot Quests provide ongoing benefits once completed. Building purchase allows adding a new action space to board and potential owner income. Resources are adventurer cubes and gold; no randomness in acquisition, so efficiency and timing dominate. Ties and timing around quest completion matter for VP races.

scoring_and_endgame.content: End after round 8. Score current VP track plus Lord bonus points based on hidden objective, plus any unresolved card/building endgame bonuses. Highest VP wins; tie-breaker most gold, then most adventurers.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Identify your Lord objective early and bias quest picks toward it without becoming predictable. 2) Waterdeep Harbor is tempo-positive when you have strong intrigue cards queued. 3) Buy buildings when they improve your own action economy first, owner bonus second. 4) Use Mandatory Quests to blunt a leader’s timing spike. 5) Avoid resource glut—turn cubes/gold into completed quests quickly.
